companyDescription:
PayFuture is a next-generation, innovative payment gateway connector that enables large e-commerce brands to access local payment solutions across emerging markets worldwide. We open the door to regions where 85% of the world’s population resides.

Through a single API integration, PayFuture allows global online merchants to unlock net new revenue streams by offering customers in traditionally hard-to-reach markets the most popular local payment options, quickly and easily.

As internet usage continues to grow in these regions, so too does the opportunity for e-commerce businesses, both local and international, to scale profitably. PayFuture provides a competitive edge through enhanced customer journeys, higher approval rates, and conversion recovery tools, all designed to increase revenue and improve performance.

By opening up access to these high-potential markets, PayFuture helps ensure long-term growth and profitability for our merchants.
companySlogan: Reach billions in emerging markets
descriptionHtml:
Role Overview

We are looking for a Marketing Director to lead Payfuture's global marketing strategy and execution. This senior leadership role will be instrumental in positioning Payfuture as the gateway of choice for merchants and clients seeking cross-border transaction solutions and banking services. Based in Dubai or London, you will oversee a high-performing team, design a go-to-market strategy, and drive brand awareness through innovative initiatives.

Key Responsibilities:

Strategy & Leadership

  • Develop and execute a go-to-market (GTM) strategy from a marketing perspective, targeting merchants and clients seeking payment gateway services, cross border solutions, or banking services
  • Build a marketing roadmap balancing quick wins with long-term industry positioning, aligned to Payfuture's growth objectives
  • Lead, mentor, and grow a high-performing marketing team across multiple regions

Brand & Market Positioning

  • Define Payfuture's unique positioning as an industry leader in emerging markets, ensuring a differentiated voice across global fintech conversations
  • Drive brand awareness through innovative and creative initiatives, exploring channels, campaigns, and partnerships that stand out from industry norms
  • Manage external communications, PR, media relations, and thought leadership initiatives to strengthen credibility

Campaigns & Demand Generation

  • Map out and execute a comprehensive marketing plan including industry events, digital campaigns, PR, and tailored sales enablement materials
  • Lead the creation of digital content, thought leadership, and performance marketing campaigns to fuel demand generation
  • Partner with the sales team to optimise lead generation, pipeline acceleration, and client acquisition

Market Intelligence & Growth

  • Conduct research to identify industry targets and opportunities in both the short term (quick wins) and long term (strategic markets)
  • Continuously track industry trends, competitor activity, and regulatory changes to inform go-to-market strategy
  • Represent Payfuture at high-profile industry events and position leadership as key voices in the sector


Requirements

Qualifications & Experience

  • Proven track record (10+ years) in senior marketing roles, ideally within fintech or payments
  • Expertise in building go-to-market strategies and positioning companies for scale in international and emerging markets
  • Demonstrated success in driving brand awareness through creative and unconventional marketing approaches
  • Strong background in digital marketing, B2B demand generation, PR, and content strategy
  • Exceptional leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills
  • Experience working across international markets, with an understanding of cultural nuances
descriptionText:
Role Overview

We are looking for a Marketing Director to lead Payfuture's global marketing strategy and execution. This senior leadership role will be instrumental in positioning Payfuture as the gateway of choice for merchants and clients seeking cross-border transaction solutions and banking services. Based in Dubai or London, you will oversee a high-performing team, design a go-to-market strategy, and drive brand awareness through innovative initiatives.

Key Responsibilities:

Strategy & Leadership

Develop and execute a go-to-market (GTM) strategy from a marketing perspective, targeting merchants and clients seeking payment gateway services, cross border solutions, or banking servicesBuild a marketing roadmap balancing quick wins with long-term industry positioning, aligned to Payfuture's growth objectivesLead, mentor, and grow a high-performing marketing team across multiple regions

Brand & Market Positioning

Campaigns & Demand Generation

Map out and execute a comprehensive marketing plan including industry events, digital campaigns, PR, and tailored sales enablement materialsLead the creation of digital content, thought leadership, and performance marketing campaigns to fuel demand generationPartner with the sales team to optimise lead generation, pipeline acceleration, and client acquisition

Market Intelligence & Growth

Conduct research to identify industry targets and opportunities in both the short term (quick wins) and long term (strategic markets)Continuously track industry trends, competitor activity, and regulatory changes to inform go-to-market strategyRepresent Payfuture at high-profile industry events and position leadership as key voices in the sector


Requirements

Qualifications & Experience

companyDescription: AKQA is a creative company at the intersection of imagination and technology, with a global presence across 19 countries, and 30 locations. For more than three decades, AKQA has pioneered what’s next. From the dawn of digital to the rise of AI, AKQA is globally recognised for its creative excellence and innovation. The company has earned top honours including the Cannes Lions International Festival of Creativity Grand Prix for Digital Craft 2023, Design and Glass 2024, and Innovation 2025, alongside seven Fast Company Innovation by Design Awards and three World Changing Ideas Awards.
descriptionHtml:
At AKQA (part of WPP), we believe in the imaginative application of art and science to create a better future. With an entrepreneurial and collaborative approach, we design adaptive ideas, services and experiences that capture the imagination, delivering impressive returns for our clients.

This role is a dual role with both account leadership and new business being at the heart of The Senior Account Director holds a senior client leadership role within AKQA. You will be responsible and accountable to the General Manager and indirectly the AKQA MENA management team for client relationships and new business development.

As a Senior Account Director in MENA, you will be responsible for building valuable long term client relationships by working with senior clients who understand the broader strategic opportunities of AKQA’s offering to their business. Your role focuses on providing strategic direction for each of your accounts and delivering imagination and innovation for the client’s business in partnership with the wider team. Your role will be particularly essential in navigating and building strong client relations with clients in the Kingdom, through work that resonates both locally as well as for audiences globally.

You will work closely with a growing, meritocratic team of talented individuals based both locally and across the MENA region. There will be the opportunity to learn and grow within a creative and technically advanced environment, with access to ongoing personal and professional development. At AKQA, we’re committed to your growth, as well as your wellbeing.

Role Requirements

  • Builds and maintains a deep and mutually beneficial client relationship; challenges clients; identifies opportunities, educates them, learns from them, understands key business issues and regularly plans and facilitates quarterly review sessions.
  • Manager escalated client / project issues - acts as an escalation point for client and project related issues.
  • Builds good relationships with team members, ensuring the team has a high level of morale.
  • Identifies key business opportunities and ensures the full breadth and depth of AKQA resources are presented.
  • Leadership and management of multi-disciplinary resources as and when they are engaged in client activity (project management, strategy, technology and creative).
  • Conducts company performance reviews, sets SMART objectives and reviews regularly.
  • Monthly financial tasks - complete monthly financial tasks such as revenue recognition and forecasting in a timely and accurate fashion.
  • Ensures compliance with all internal account and project tracking procedures .
  • Ensures client projects/activity is processed/billed in a timely and accurate manner.
  • Regularly and consistently maintains reporting documents to manage clients and projects efficiently and professionally; forecasting, resourcing, status reporting and invoicing.
  • Meets and exceeds revenue targets.
  • High quality of project deliverables meets deadlines and works within estimations.
  • Ensures projects are delivered in a timely and professional manner taking responsibility and accountability for all internal resources and costs used on client work.
  • Ensures direct reports are meeting utilisation and billability targets, completing timesheets accurately.
  • Fluent in English, both spoken and written. Fluency in spoken Arabic required.
  • Previous experience in Saudi Arabia and/or the MENA region required.

QUALITIES AND CHARACTERISTICS

  • Extensive account management experience at a leading digital agency, professional services firm or similar organisation.
  • Successful track record of building exceptional client relationships; necessary capability, presence and professionalism to inspire confidence and credibility with clients and colleagues.
  • Experience in new business development, creating connections and responding to regional RFPs.
  • Understanding and experience of web/internet and marketing; experience in business strategy, information design, creative design and web technology (what we sell), as well as broad understanding of marketing/advertising elements of campaigns, annual plans, strategic goals and brand awareness.
  • A passion for innovation and business strategy.
  • Ability to build successful ‘trusted advisor’ relationships with key senior client contacts.

We believe great work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. That's why we’ve adopted a hybrid approach including required in-office days. If you require accommodations or flexibility, please discuss this with the hiring team during the interview process.

AKQA is an equal opportunity employer and considers applicants for all positions without discrimination or regard to particular characteristics. We are committed to fostering a culture of respect in which everyone feels they belong and has the same opportunities to progress in their careers.

About WPP

WPP is the trusted growth partner for the world’s leading brands. We unite cutting-edge media intelligence and data solutions, world-class creativity, next-generation production, transformative enterprise solutions and expert strategic counsel in a single company – powered by exceptional talent and our agentic marketing platform, WPP Open, to help our clients navigate change, capture opportunity and deliver transformational growth. For more information, visit WPP.com.

descriptionHtml:
Director of Sales & Marketing, for a luxury hotel - based in Mongolia

(Mandatory Experience: Luxury Independent Hotels work history or i.e. Leading Hotels LHW or small luxury Hotels ETC.


We are seeking an accomplished, very hands on, and strategic Director of Sales & Marketing to lead the commercial vision, brand positioning, and revenue growth of a new premier luxury hotel in Mongolia. This high-profile leadership role is instrumental in establishing the property as a flagship destination for international travelers and discerning local clientele. As this is an independent property, the Director of Sales & Marketing should know everything about Sales & marketing without having to call head office for assistance.


Key Responsibilities

  • Lead the strategic positioning of the hotel, shaping a compelling brand identity aligned with global luxury standards
  • Develop and execute integrated sales and marketing strategies to drive revenue, market share, and brand recognition
  • Oversee all sales segments including leisure, corporate, MICE, and high-net-worth individual markets
  • Spearhead innovative marketing campaigns, digital presence, partnerships, and PR initiatives targeting key international and regional markets
  • Ensure exceptional quality of execution across all commercial functions, with a strong focus on follow-through and measurable performance outcomes
  • Identify and implement new revenue opportunities, including luxury experiences and bespoke offerings
  • Lead the development, launch, or enhancement of a high-end private club or membership program within the hotel or affiliated lifestyle platforms
  • Build, mentor, and inspire a high-performing sales and marketing team, fostering accountability and collaboration
  • Work closely with Rooms, F&B, Operations, and Finance teams to ensure alignment of commercial strategies with overall guest experience and operational excellence
  • Establish and maintain strong relationships with key partners, travel advisors, agencies, and corporate clients across Asia and globally

Qualifications & Experience

  • Minimum 8–10 years of progressive leadership experience in sales and marketing within the luxury hospitality sector
  • Proven track record of success in Asia, with deep understanding of regional markets and luxury consumer behavior
  • Demonstrated experience in launching or managing a high-end private club or membership program within a hospitality or lifestyle environment
  • Strong commercial acumen with a results-driven mindset and hands-on approach to execution
  • Exceptional leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills, with a collaborative working style across departments
  • Creative thinker with the ability to introduce innovative ideas and translate them into actionable strategies
  • High attention to detail, accountability, and commitment to excellence in performance
  • Fluency in English is required; additional languages such as Mandarin, Cantonese, or Korean are highly advantageous

Personal Attributes

  • Strategic, entrepreneurial, and performance-oriented
  • Culturally aware with a global mindset and appreciation for local market dynamics
  • Adaptable, resilient, and capable of thriving in a dynamic, high-growth environment
  • Passionate about luxury hospitality and delivering exceptional guest experiences


To Apply:

Please send your CV and a brief cover letter highlighting your relevant experience to Mr. Steve Renard, President at steve@renardinternational.com


Best regards,

Stephen J. Renard

President

Renard International Hospitality Search Consultants

Recipient of the 31st Annual Pinnacle Awards: "Hotelier Magazine, 2019 Supplier of the Year" https://www.hoteliermagazine.com/2019-pinnacle-awards-renard/

"We use our reputation to help build yours. Celebrating over five decades of Excellence - since 1970"

121 Richmond Street West, Suite 601

Toronto, ON. Canada, M5H 2K1

companyDescription: Fortis is a UAE-based retail tech company revolutionizing how SMEs operate in MENA. In partnership with Network International, we transform card machines into powerful POS systems, combining seamless sales, payments, and e-commerce with loyalty programs and real-time analytics. Headquartered in Dubai, we help SMEs thrive in the digital era by uniting offline and online operations.
companySlogan: Empowering SMEs with an Integrated Online and Offline Business Platform
descriptionHtml:
Fortis Digital Solutions is a fast-growing fintech company based in the UAE, focused on empowering small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs) with modern digital tools to manage and grow their businesses.


Our integrated platform enables merchants to accept payments, manage business operations, and access financial solutions—all in one place. By simplifying payments and improving operational efficiency, we help merchants scale faster and unlock new revenue opportunities. Fortis is on a mission to digitize SMB commerce across the region, and we are building a world-class sales organization to accelerate this growth.


We are looking for an experienced Sales Director to lead and scale our commercial organization in the UAE. This role is responsible for driving revenue growth and merchant acquisition while building a high-performing and scalable sales team. Sales Director will design and execute the company’s sales strategy across inbound conversion, outbound acquisition, and account management, ensuring strong pipeline generation, high conversion rates, and sustainable revenue growth.


This role requires a hands-on leader with deep experience in payments and SMB sales in the UAE market, as well as a proven track record in building and managing high-performing outbound sales teams.


Key Responsibilities

Revenue Growth & Sales Performance

  • Own overall revenue targets and SMB merchant acquisition goals
  • Establish clear sales KPIs and performance metrics across the sales organization
  • Drive disciplined pipeline management, forecasting, and reporting
  • Monitor funnel performance and conversion metrics
  • Conduct weekly and monthly sales performance reviews
  • Identify gaps in performance and implement corrective actions

Sales Organization Leadership

  • Design and build a scalable sales organization structure
  • Hire, develop, and manage high-performing sales leaders and individual contributors
  • Build leadership across key teams:
  • Inbound Sales
  • Outbound Sales
  • Account Management
  • Implement performance management frameworks and coaching programs
  • Foster a culture of accountability, ownership, and results

Inbound Lead Conversion

  • Own the conversion of inbound leads into paying merchants
  • Work closely with the Marketing team to optimize lead conversion performance
  • Define lead qualification frameworks and routing processes
  • Establish response time and follow-up standards
  • Implement inbound sales playbooks and best practices

Outbound Sales Strategy

  • Design and scale a structured outbound sales engine
  • Define Ideal Customer Profiles (ICP) and market segmentation
  • Develop outbound prospecting strategies and messaging
  • Build scalable outreach frameworks and sales playbooks
  • Establish activity and pipeline KPIs
  • Monitor outbound pipeline contribution and performance

Account Management & Expansion

  • Build a scalable account management model focused on retention and growth
  • Establish processes for renewals, upselling, and cross-selling
  • Implement Quarterly Business Reviews (QBRs) with key clients
  • Drive adoption of additional Fortis products
  • Work closely with Customer Success and Support teams


Must-Have Require

  • 10+ years of sales experience with at least 5+ years in a senior sales leadership role.
  • Proven experience in the payments, fintech, or merchant acquiring industry
  • Strong experience selling solutions to SME/SMB clients in the UAE market
  • Demonstrated experience building, structuring, and scaling high-performing sales teams
  • Proven track record of leading outbound sales organizations and building predictable pipeline generation
  • Consistent history of achieving and exceeding revenue and merchant acquisition targets
  • Strong understanding of the UAE SME ecosystem and merchant land scape
  • Experience working cross-functionally with marketing, partnership, and customer success teams to drive revenue growth


As part of our recruitment process, we process candidate personal data for recruitment and selection purposes. This may include recording or transcription of interviews, AI-assisted summarisation of interview notes, and storage of candidate information in our applicant tracking system. These tools support our recruitment team and do not make final hiring decisions without human review. We retain candidate information only for as long as necessary for the relevant recruitment process, unless you separately agree that we may keep your details for future opportunities.


By submitting your application, you acknowledge that your personal data will be processed in accordance with this notice.

descriptionHtml:
As the Regional Director of Sales, you will play a pivotal role in driving revenue growth and market expansion across the MENA region. Leveraging your extensive sales leadership experience, you will be responsible for developing and executing regional sales strategies, managing key corporate accounts, and guiding property-level sales teams. Your focus on optimizing sales performance, identifying new opportunities, and fostering strong partnerships will directly contribute to the success of the group’s hotels and residences.

Key Job Responsibilities:

  • Develop and implement regional-level sales strategies aligned with group objectives across the MENA region.
  • Provide leadership and guidance to property sales teams, ensuring consistency and best practices across all markets.
  • Conduct regional market analysis to identify growth opportunities and evolving trends.
  • Build and maintain strong relationships with key corporate and regional accounts, partners, and industry stakeholders.
  • Oversee negotiations for MENA-wide contracts, partnerships, and preferred agreements.
  • Collaborate with revenue management to align sales initiatives with pricing and distribution strategies.
  • Work closely with corporate marketing and communications teams to create impactful regional sales campaigns and collateral.
  • Monitor sales performance across properties, providing insights, forecasts, and reports to senior leadership.
  • Establish KPIs and review performance of property sales teams, providing coaching and support as required.
  • Drive participation in international and regional exhibitions, trade shows, roadshows, and networking events to represent the brand portfolio.
  • Support pre-opening projects by defining and executing sales strategies for new properties in the region.
  • Ensure alignment of sales activities with brand positioning, business goals, and compliance requirements.
  • Promote collaboration between sales, operations, and other corporate functions to enhance guest and client experiences.
  • Manage the corporate sales budget, monitoring expenses and ensuring cost-effective strategies.
  • Stay abreast of industry best practices and incorporate innovations into corporate sales initiatives.
  • Champion sustainability and corporate responsibility in all sales activities.
  • Operate in a safe and environmentally conscious manner and adhere to company health, safety, and environmental policies.

Develop and implement regional-level sales strategies aligned with group objectives across the MENA region.Provide leadership and guidance to property sales teams, ensuring consistency and best practices across all markets.Conduct regional market analysis to identify growth opportunities and evolving trends.Build and maintain strong relationships with key corporate and regional accounts, partners, and industry stakeholders.Oversee negotiations for MENA-wide contracts, partnerships, and preferred agreements.Collaborate with revenue management to align sales initiatives with pricing and distribution strategies.Work closely with corporate marketing and communications teams to create impactful regional sales campaigns and collateral.Monitor sales performance across properties, providing insights, forecasts, and reports to senior leadership.Establish KPIs and review performance of property sales teams, providing coaching and support as required.Drive participation in international and regional exhibitions, trade shows, roadshows, and networking events to represent the brand portfolio.Support pre-opening projects by defining and executing sales strategies for new properties in the region.Ensure alignment of sales activities with brand positioning, business goals, and compliance requirements.Promote collaboration between sales, operations, and other corporate functions to enhance guest and client experiences.Manage the corporate sales budget, monitoring expenses and ensuring cost-effective strategies.Stay abreast of industry best practices and incorporate innovations into corporate sales initiatives.Champion sustainability and corporate responsibility in all sales activities.Operate in a safe and environmentally conscious manner and adhere to company health, safety, and environmental policies.
title: Regional Director of Sales

companyDescription:
The American Hospital Dubai is a 254-bed private hospital providing healthcare to the highest US standards and is the leader and pioneer in international accreditation and recognition. 

The hospital serves patients across the region and includes several specialist centers – Comprehensive Cancer Center, Orthopedics and Total Joint Replacement Center for adults, The Heart Center, Diabetes Program, and Cochlear Implant Center. 

All our physicians are Western Board Certified. The American Hospital Satellite Clinics in Dubai Media City, Al Barsha, Al Khawaneej, Jumeirah, Mira, Dubai Hills & Nad Al Sheba now offer the same quality of care within the community. 

The hospital is the first Middle East member of the prestigious Mayo Clinic Care Network.

descriptionHtml:
POSITION SUMMARY:

Provides strategic leadership for all digital marketing, digital transformation, and patient engagement initiatives. Leads high-impact digital growth strategies to enhance patient acquisition, experience, and brand leadership.


STATEMENT OF VALUES:

An employee of the American Hospital Dubai is expected to promote the mission and philosophy of the hospital by acting in a caring, courteous and confidential manner towards patients, visitors, colleagues and staff by performing their duties and responsibilities according to the expected standards in order to fully meet patient care needs.


DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

Brand & Reputation

•Defines and leads enterprise digital marketing and transformation strategy aligned with organizational vision across all centers of excellence.

•Drives omnichannel patient acquisition, engagement, and retention strategies using data-driven insights.

•Oversees full digital ecosystem including websites, CRM, marketing automation, and integrations.


Growth & Partnerships

•Directs large-scale digital campaigns across SEO, SEM, PPC, social media, and content platforms.

•Drives patient experience enhancements through digital touchpoints and journey optimization.

•Builds strategic partnerships with global agencies, technology providers, and healthcare platforms.


Governance, Compliance & Risk

•Establishes governance frameworks for digital marketing performance, ROI, and KPI tracking.

•Works with Quality & Governance teams to integrate patient experience into performance indicators.


People & Financial Leadership

•Promotes a culture of accountability, creativity, and excellence.

•Manages budgets ensuring efficiency, ROI, and financial governance.


QUALIFICATIONS & SKILLS:

•Bachelor’s Degree in Marketing, Business Administration, Communications, or related field.

•Master’s Degree in Business Administration (MBA), Marketing, or Innovation preferred.

•Certifications in digital transformation, brand management, or customer experience preferred.


PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE:

•Minimum 10 years of progressive experience in digital marketing with at least 3 years in leadership roles.

•Proven track record in digital transformation and large-scale marketing initiatives.

•Knowledge of brand strategy, corporate communications, and reputation management in service-driven sectors.

•Knowledge of UAE healthcare regulations, accreditation standards, and patient experience frameworks.

•Skills in strategic planning, growth initiatives, and partnership development.

•Skills in digital transformation, CRM, marketing automation, and omnichannel engagement.

•Ability to influence and inspire at executive level, translating strategy into measurable outcomes.

companyDescription:
Artelia is an international multidisciplinary consultancy, engineering and project management group specialising in mobility, water, energy, building and industry. 
 
Artelia is offering a full range of engineering from technical expertise through to complex project delivery: consulting, master planning & feasibility, design & engineering, construction & project management, asset & facility management, turnkey solutions.
 
Our Raison d’être: Designing solutions for a positive life
 
We are united by a common passion, exercising a profession that combines the art of designing with ingenuity and building with commitment.
 
Artelia is wholly-owned by its staff and managers. We are deeply attached to this shareholding model which promotes long-term vision, creates a spirit of ownership and guarantees our independence.
This development model is expressed in the projects we carry out in response to the challenges of our times: resilience to climate change, energy transition, sustainable use of resources, advanced industrial facilities, regeneration of the built environment, more livable cities, multimodal mobility. In all these areas, our duty is to offer solutions that are both innovative and realistic, bold and tangible, to help us, as citizens of the world, to lead a positive life.
 
With 9,700 employees and a turnover of €934m in 2022, Artelia is in the top 15 consulting engineering firms in Europe. Internationally, the Group carries out projects in more than 100 countries and has offices in 40 countries in Europe, Africa, the Middle East, Asia-Pacific and the Americas.
descriptionHtml:
1 Mission

The Project Director is in charge of the complete implementation of the management strategy for projects under his/her responsibility, and for which he/she has the support of resources either inside or outside the affiliate.

To meet the expectations of Clients, for whom he/she is the direct and key contact, the Project Director supervises architectural and technical studies. Every decision of design modification leading to financial consequences is taken by him/her. At the same time, he/she guarantees the economic balance of the project and the respect of Quality principles. He/she also deals with all the contractual issues between Artelia Airports and its clients.

In the management of studies, the Project Director supervises the project teams made available. He/she draws up and manages design contracts entered into with designers outside Artelia Airports and guarantees their functioning. With the support of the functional resources of affiliates, he/she defines the subcontracting strategy, then drafts and manages the subcontracting contracts necessary to project implementation.

He/She contributes to the definition of the affiliate’s strategic focus.


2 Line and Staff Relations

The role will directly report to the Director, Production and Operations and will work in close collaboration with the following teams:


  • ARTELIA AIRPORTS Staff
  • Design Manager
  • Contract Administrator
  • Director of Tendering and Consulting
  • Resident Engineer
  • Planning Engineer
  • Design Team, all disciplines
  • Supervision Team at site
  • QA/QC
  • HSE
  • External Collaborators
  • Client Representatives
  • Sub-Consultants
  • Contractors / Sub-Contractors


3 Key Responsibilities

Ensures the management of signed contracts

  • He/she negotiates the technical and financial approach to projects in his/her portfolio (with Management assisted by the Business Development)
  • He/she defines the progress of the project (consistent with the Client’s specifications) and the recourse to outside resources (subcontracting)
  • He/she defines the technical, budgetary and financial resources necessary
  • He/she defines resources to be implemented and monitoring indicators
  • He/she monitors Management’s continuous and adequate information as far as finance, technical choices and deadlines are concerned, in providing the Administrative, Financial and Human Resources Director and the Director of Production and Operations with the data necessary to their audit (Control Panels and performance indicators)
  • He/she uses feedback information from the Administrative, Financial and Human Resources Director and the Director of Production and Operations in the management of his project
  • He/she supervises studies and manages teams:
  • breaks specifications down into study objectives and study team needs
  • establishes the monitoring indicators of studies to be carried out
  • directs, focuses and coordinates the group of project contributors, i.e. :
  • is in charge of teams under his/her direct responsibility.
  • focuses and coordinates the work of outside designers.
  • coordinates the work of teams, including those teams working on the site.
  • monitors subcontractors.
  • monitors Artelia Airports spending and manages it in relation to projected financial schedules.
  • guarantees project / Client program appropriateness (functional and technical aspects, study and finishing deadlines and target costs)
  • monitors Quality and the respect of project deadlines through continuous dialogue with outside designers (optimization of economic choices, project consistency)
  • contributes to the concrete production of studies
  • updates the Director of Operations and Production on technical bases
  • He/she manages Client relations:
  • conducts the technical dialogue on the project with the Client (technical choices, functionality)
  • validates the various stages of the project with the Client
  • conducts the closure of services due under contracts entered into with Clients
  • implements the contracts entered into with the Clients
  • monitors the deadlines, quality and costs follow-up
  • is responsible to report to executive management any changes of the Artelia Airports contract
  • prepares, with support from the Business Development Manager, the technical and financial files for possible complaints (follow-up letters etc…)
  • advises the Business Development Manager and Executive Management in terms on market opportunities when required
  • He/she updates load plans by:
  • Defining staff needs according to their skills
  • Allowing or denying additional missions
  • Accepting vacations
  • Managing all the means needed for his project
  • Contractors:
  • signs all the instructions to the contractor including:
  • notice to proceed and eventually to stop the works on site
  • technical variation orders
  • financial variation orders
  • schedule variation orders
  • penalty orders for delay
  • validates all the contractors’ submissions and quality control plans produced by contractors or he/she can delegate such responsibility.
  • He/she follows the project for Artelia Airports by :
  • Following its spending and invoices
  • Approving the schedule for the project
  • Approving the missions and reporting it to the Managing Director
  • Within the framework of his activities, the Project Director contributes, in a relevant manner, to the fulfillment of Quality objectives.


4 Profile Specifications – Minimum Requirements

  • Bachelor's or Master's Degree in airport management, planning, aviation science, or engineering.
  • 15 years’ experience minimum on large size construction projects or similar transportation projects in a similar position, with a minimum of 3 airport projects references strongly preferred - both design and site supervision.
  • Thorough knowledge of engineering design, construction estimating, facility operation, project budgeting and scheduling.
  • Demonstrated knowledge of capital planning and assessment, cost-benefit analysis, planning and evaluation, and risk management protocols
  • Experienced in Project Management and FIDIC contracts
  • Experience in ICAO and IATA regulations is a plus
  • Excellent presentation, coordination and leadership skills
  • Knowledge of local laws and regulations, international codes and standards
  • Fluent in English; reading, writing and speaking
  • Proficiency in the use of standard Microsoft Office products, Primavera (Oracle’s Project Management software) or MS Project.
  • Eligibility for frequent travels to be expected.
  • SoE membership and AUH Engineering license are mandatory or proven eligibility


Note to external recruiters: Please note that Artelia Airports does not accept unsolicited resumes from recruiters or employment agencies. In the absence of a signed Recruitment Fee Agreement, Artelia Airports will not consider or agree to payment of any referral compensation or recruiter fee. In the event a recruiter or agency submits a resume or candidate without a previously signed agreement, Artelia Airports explicitly reserves the right to pursue and hire those candidate(s) without any financial obligation to the recruiter or agency. Any unsolicited resumes, including those submitted to hiring managers, are deemed to be the property of Artelia Airports and no fee will be paid in the event the candidate is hired by Artelia Airports as a result of the referral or through other means.

companyDescription:
Madison Pearl (MP) is the search firm of choice amongst the leading financial services firms and legal firms for mid to senior level positions including C-suite searches. 

Founded in 2015, we are a truly Global Executive Search Firm strategically based out of DIFC, UAE to focus on mid-senior level positions including C suite in the rapidly evolving regional financial markets; as well as developed markets such as US and Europe. We also have an office in Bahrain as an extension to our commitment to the region and to be close to our clients in Saudi Arabia & Kuwait. From our office in Dublin we cater to our clients in US and Europe. We service our APAC clients from our Hong Kong office. We also recently opened our first UK office in London.

Encompassing a wide umbrella of services including Contingent and Retained searches aswell as Market Intelligence, Compensation Benchmarking, Board Advisory and Leadership Consulting, we provide 360 degree customized solutions for our client’s Talent Acquisition, Succession Planning and Employee Retention needs.

We work in teams where knowledge is shared and the client solution discussed thoroughly from every angle. Our sector specialist consultants are passionate about providing comprehensive yet timely recruitment solutions to our clients ranging from a single key hire to an entire team build up, leveraging their solid knowledge and relationships in vertical markets.

Driving support from our dedicated in-house research team that meticulously devises a step by step search strategy to identify and attract talent pool via sourcing from our robust internal database coupled with head-hunting techniques. 

Exhibiting an unrivalled track record in successfully placing and mobilizing world class talent from leading financial & banking firms from Developed, Emerging, Frontier &rapidly developing markets, our reputation for delivery is exemplary which has earned us the confidence of our clients’ as the most trusted go to search partner.

descriptionHtml:
Madison Pearl has been mandated to lead a confidential search for a Chief Investment Officer on behalf of a reputable semi government investment manager based in the UAE. Reporting directly to executive leadership, the Chief Investment Officer will be responsible for driving investment strategy, transaction origination, strategic partnerships, and capital attraction initiatives across the platform. The successful candidate will play a central role in engaging with global investors, multinational corporates, and global contributors to identify opportunities that support long term growth, and international expansion objectives.


The organization is seeking an individual with a strong international network, exceptional commercial judgement, and a proven track record advising governments and institutional investors on complex transactions and strategic investments. This is a rare opportunity for a senior “top tier investment banking professional” to transition from sell side advisory to a leadership position supporting long term economic development.


Key Responsibilities:


  • Lead the development and execution of the organization’s investment strategy across priority sectors and markets
  • Originate and execute strategic investment opportunities, partnerships, and large-scale transactions
  • Engage directly with institutional investors, multinational corporates, and global financial institutions
  • Advise executive leadership on market trends, capital flows, and strategic growth opportunities
  • Support cross border investment and commercial development initiatives
  • Evaluate complex investment structures, financing solutions, and strategic partnerships
  • Represent the organization at high level international meetings, forums, and negotiations
  • Drive collaboration across public and private sector stakeholders to achieve long term strategic objectives


Key Requirements:


  • Senior investment banking leadership experience gained within a top tier international investment bank
  • Strong background in M&A, corporate finance, strategic advisory, capital raising, or principal investments
  • Deep understanding of international markets and strategic capital deployment
  • Exceptional stakeholder engagement and executive communication capabilities
  • Experience operating within emerging markets and international growth environments is highly advantageous
  • Bachelor’s degree required; advanced academic qualifications preferred


#ChiefInvestmentOfficer #InvestmentBanking #CorporateFinance #MergersAndAcquisitions #FDI #SovereignWealth #PrivateCapital #GCC #MiddleEast #ExecutiveSearch


Interested candidates please apply to this advertisement directly or send your CV to aashish.gidwani@madisonpearl.com



*Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted*

descriptionHtml:
We are seeking an experienced Sales Director(UAEN) with deep expertise across freight forwarding, logistics services, commercial sales (B2B & B2C), and strategic sector engagement. The ideal candidate will drive significant revenue growth, lead high-impact sales strategies, and manage major customer relationships across freight, supply chain, pharma, oil & gas, materials, F&B, and government accounts. This role is critical in expanding the organization’s commercial footprint in the UAE and GCC, serving as a key leader in both field sales execution and strategic market development.



Key Responsibilities


Strategic Sales & Commercial Leadership

  • Develop, implement, and lead end-to-end sales strategies for freight, logistics, and sector portfolios across both B2B and B2C customer segments
  • Set and execute annual and quarterly commercial targets with full accountability for revenue, market share, and profitability
  • Lead strategic pricing, proposal development, and contract negotiations with major enterprise and government clients
  • Build and manage a strong pipeline of new business while expanding relationships with high-value existing accounts


Customer & Market Engagement

  • Act as the principal commercial representative in customer engagements, delivering solution-based approaches for freight forwarding, logistics contracts, warehousing, and cross-border shipping services
  • Lead market analysis, competitor research, and commercial intelligence to support go-to-market strategies
  • Represent the organization at industry forums, exhibitions, trade shows, and senior stakeholder meetings


Sales Execution & Operations

  • Lead and manage direct sales teams and channel partners to ensure high performance against KPIs and delivery of customer commitments
  • Collaborate closely with operations, customer service, pricing, CRM, and digital teams to ensure seamless execution from lead generation through service delivery
  • Oversee CRM governance, pipeline reporting, forecasting accuracy, and sales performance dashboards


B2B & B2C Sales Coverage

  • Drive B2B commercial growth with large corporates, government entities, 3PL/4PL providers, manufacturers, and strategic accounts across priority sectors
  • Lead B2C engagement strategies where applicable (e-commerce logistics, consumer freight solutions, large customer segments)
  • Develop customer segmentation, retention, and expansion strategies to maximize lifetime customer value


Essential Competencies & Skills

  • Industry & Commercial Acumen: Strong knowledge of freight forwarding, logistics services (air/sea/land), supply chain solutions, and UAE market dynamics
  • Sales Leadership & Strategy: Proven ability to lead teams, drive performance, and deliver sustained commercial growth
  • Stakeholder Management: Ability to engage credibly with C-suite executives, government officials, and strategic partners
  • Analytical & Market Insight: Strong capability to interpret market trends, competitor behavior, and customer data
  • Communication & Negotiation: Excellent presentation, influencing, and negotiation skills



Experience & Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, Supply Chain, Logistics, or a related discipline (MBA or professional certifications preferred)
  • Minimum 10+ years of progressive sales experience, with at least 5 years in senior sales leadership roles within freight forwarding, logistics, or related industries
  • Demonstrated success across B2B enterprise sales and B2C market engagement, including government and multinational accounts
  • Sector exposure to logistics providers, ports, freight operators, pharma, oil & gas, materials, and F&B ecosystems
  • Proven track record of exceeding sales targets, closing large contracts, and driving growth in competitive markets

At Pollux, we build The Intelligent Enterprise OS — a connected suite of AI-powered products that help modern businesses run smarter. From Sales CRM, Creator Commerce, Enterprise Commerce and PLM to Logistics, WMS, our platform enables organizations to automate decisions, streamline operations, and scale with precision.

Every Pollux product can operate independently or integrate seamlessly for end-to-end enterprise intelligence. Whether it’s optimizing supply chains, unifying customer experiences, or empowering partner-driven sales, Pollux provides the intelligence layer for sustainable business growth.

With enterprise-grade flexibility, Pollux empowers you to move faster, optimize processes, and grow with confidence.

companyDescription:
Headquartered in Hong Kong SAR, the Shangri-La Group has grown from a single hotel business to a diverse and integrated global portfolio comprising quality real estate and investment properties, wellness and lifestyle facilities. Today, the Group owns, operates and manages 100+ hotels under our family of five brands: Shangri-La, Shangri-La Signatures, Kerry Hotels, JEN by Shangri-La, and Traders.

We are part of the Kuok Group, one of Asia's most dynamic multinational conglomerates and a leader in properties, logistics, agribusiness, maritime and hospitality.

From our strong base in Asia, we have expanded into key gateway cities and markets around the world. Our properties sit on some of the world’s most prestigious addresses and exotic destinations. Through the environments we have created, we enable people to come together to live, work, play, eat, and rest well.
companySlogan: Find Your Shangri-La in Shangri-La
descriptionHtml:
About Us

Find Your Shangri-La in Shangri-La

Shangri-La Dubai is a luxurious urban sanctuary where modern energy meets timeless hospitality. With panoramic views of the Burj Khalifa and Dubai’s glittering skyline, every visit becomes an unforgettable experience.

About The Role

As the Director of Sales, you will be responsible for overseeing the daily operations and performance of the Sales Department. Working closely with the Sales leadership team, you will execute the hotel’s sales and marketing strategies. Additionally, you will lead sales initiatives and actively drive revenue growth by identifying and maximizing sales opportunities.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead the implementation of the hotel’s sales policies, procedures, and brand standards in alignment with Shangri-La Hotels and Resorts’ guidelines.
  • Develop and recommend optimal contracted room rate structures across all segments to maximize revenue, drive RevPAR, and exceed budgeted targets.
  • Continuously analyze market trends, competitor pricing, and sales strategies to maintain a leading market position and inform strategic sales decisions.
  • Collaborate with the Sales Leadership to develop and execute segment-focused sales and marketing strategies, actively contributing to revenue strategy meetings.
  • Build, lead, and motivate a high-performing sales team through structured recruitment, performance standards, and regular evaluations to consistently exceed revenue expectations.
  • Ensure strict adherence to Shangri-La Account Management standards, Delphi database integrity, and effective use of sales and revenue systems to optimize revenue opportunities.
  • Manage sales expense budgets, oversee travel planning and expenses, set objectives for sales trips, and evaluate outcomes to maximize sales productivity and return on investment.
  • Focus on total revenue generation including rooms,- F&B and banquets revenues.
  • Collaborate with Revenue Management and Marketing teams to develop promotions, packages, and campaigns targeted at the corporate segment.
  • Maintain accurate records of all sales activities in the CRM system.
  • Attend industry trade shows, conferences, and networking events to enhance the hotel’s visibility and develop professional relationships.
  • Work closely with the operations team to ensure seamless delivery of service for corporate clients.

About You

  • Experience in Sales & Marketing in an international 5* luxury property.
  • Hold a business-related educational background.
  • Be self-driven, goal-oriented, willing to challenge the status quo.
  • Uphold professional values, ethics, and integrity always.
  • Have the ability to network, building strong & meaningful business relationships.
  • Have experience in the local market (UAE as well as other GCC).
  • Communicate and write with fluency in English (as well as the local language).
  • Have strong selling, interpersonal and communication skills.

Why Join Us

  • A workplace that values your passion and supports self-realization and personal growth.
  • Structured learning and development pathways with real opportunities to advance your professional craft and leadership skills.
  • Competitive benefits, recognition programs, and colleague stay/travel perks that reward your contribution and dedication.
  • Teams that promote inclusion and respect, value diversity, and foster a secure environment where everyone can thrive.

We are an equal opportunity employer. Applications from all qualified candidates are welcomed. All information provided by applicants will be treated in and used only for recruitment purposes.

We appreciate your interest in joining us. Please note that only successful candidates will be contacted.

Halian is seeking an experienced senior leader to take full ownership of our DACH region, leading Sales, Managed Services, and Operations across Germany, Austria, and Switzerland.

descriptionHtml:
Group Marketing Director – Dubai, UAE


We are partnering with a well-established investment group/family office based in Dubai to appoint a Group Marketing Director. This is a rare opportunity to lead and unify the marketing strategy across a diversified portfolio of businesses spanning multiple sectors.


The Role

Reporting directly to senior leadership, the Group Marketing Director will be responsible for building and executing a cohesive, group-wide marketing vision while supporting the commercial objectives of individual business units. You will play a key role in elevating brand positioning, driving growth, and creating synergy across the group’s various verticals.


Key Responsibilities

  • Develop and implement a group-level marketing strategy aligned with business goals
  • Oversee brand architecture, ensuring consistency and clarity across multiple entities
  • Lead and mentor marketing teams across different business units
  • Drive digital transformation, performance marketing, and data-led decision-making
  • Manage agency relationships, budgets, and campaign execution
  • Identify cross-selling and collaboration opportunities across the portfolio
  • Support new ventures, launches, and strategic investments with go-to-market plans


Requirements

  • Proven experience in a senior marketing leadership role, ideally within a group structure, holding company, or multi-brand environment
  • Strong track record across brand, digital, and performance marketing
  • Experience working within or alongside investment groups, family offices, or diversified businesses is highly advantageous
  • Exceptional stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence at board level
  • Commercially driven, strategic thinker with a hands-on approach
  • Experience in the Middle East market, particularly the UAE, is preferred


What’s on Offer

  • Opportunity to shape the marketing function of a highly respected, diversified investment group
  • Exposure to multiple industries and high-impact strategic initiatives
  • Competitive executive package, aligned with experience


If you are a strategic marketing leader looking to make a broad and lasting impact across a dynamic portfolio, we would welcome a confidential discussion.

descriptionHtml:
Director | Issues & Crisis Management | Dammam, KSA


We are partnering with a leading global communications consultancy to appoint a Director — Issues & Crisis Management based in Dammam, KSA. This is a senior advisory role supporting high-profile regional and international clients on business-critical mandates, reputation risk, and complex stakeholder environments.


The Director will play a central role in anticipating, managing, and responding to reputational, regulatory, and operational issues — providing strategic counsel to C-suite leaders and government stakeholders across sensitive, fast-moving situations.


Key Responsibilities

  • Lead end-to-end issues and crisis management mandates for major corporate and government clients
  • Advise senior executives on reputation risk, crisis preparedness, and response strategy
  • Develop scenario planning frameworks, holding statements, Q&A, and escalation protocols
  • Direct rapid-response media strategy and stakeholder communications during live issues
  • Provide counsel on geopolitical, regulatory, and policy-driven developments impacting clients
  • Manage cross-market teams and coordinate with legal, compliance, and public affairs advisors
  • Support business development through crisis audits, risk assessments, and client proposals


Candidate Profile

  • 10–15+ years’ experience in issues management, crisis communications, or strategic advisory
  • Background in a global agency, advisory firm, government entity, or in-house corporate affairs team
  • Proven experience handling high-stakes, reputation-critical situations
  • Strong understanding of the Saudi / GCC media, regulatory, and stakeholder landscape
  • Exceptional executive presence and stakeholder management skills
  • Experience advising C-suite and government stakeholders is essential
  • Arabic language capability is advantageous but not mandatory

descriptionHtml:
SALES DIRECTOR – REAL ESTATE – Dubai - AED 30K - 35K + Family Status


Our client, one of the Leading Semi-govt Real Estate Developer is looking to hire Sales Director based in Dubai. Budget; AED 30K - 35K + Family Status (Visa, Medical, Annual Airfare + Education allowance) + Commission, Reporting to – Executive VP Sales & Operations.


Note: Only candidates with Developer experience will be shortlisted, Managing Team experience is a must. The role will be managing a team of Sales Managers.


Job Summary: Organize, direct, and manage the sales and leasing activities to achieve set revenue targets within budgeted costs. Motivate the team, develop, and implement leasing and sales procedures. Oversee the completion of all necessary paperwork and ensure proper customer service.

Key responsibility:

  1. Manage the daily activities of the sales and leasing team in achieving their goals as set by the HOS.
  2. Meet individual targets and Team targets.
  3. Assist HOD in developing plans for team sales activities to include sales strategy to achieve agreed targets, effective search of leads/prospects, penetrate new markets
  4. Prepare and agree upon individual action plans and targets for all team members
  5. Recruit, Train, and Coach the Sales Team and monitor their performance.
  6. Motivate Sales Team by Listening to their feedback and resolve any issues or conflicts
  7. Recognize high performance and reward accomplishments
  8. Ensure following the sales & leasing guidelines and follow the pricing structure as approved.
  9. Prepare presentations, proposals, and price lists to enable the team to deliver accurate and competitive information whilst maintaining maximum profit margin and assist team members to prepare and deliver client/customer presentations, proposals, and price lists
  10. Set an example for team members of commitment, sales activities, work ethics and habits and personal character
  11. Achieve the set financial target & meet the approved budget
  12. Interact and co-operate with all members of the organization and its departments and clients/customers
  13. Performs any other related job duties as requested by direct supervisor.


Education Qualification & Experience:


  • Bachelor’s degree in any discipline OR MBA Preferred
  • 6-8 Years of Real Estate experience in Sales with Developer.
  • Real estate agent’s or broker’s license
  • Excellent communication and negotiation skills with command over written and spoken English.
  • MS Office & other computer application, Negotiation and communication skills

companyDescription:
Anton Oilfield Services Group (hereinafter as “Anton”)  is the world's leading integrated oilfield technology service company that provides clients with a full range of products and services for oil and gas development, with business throughout the world's major oil and gas production areas. Anton is an innovative company combined with the geological engineering and features production increase, cost reduction and integration.
Anton is committed to the in-depth development of global oil and gas development in emerging markets. Its business covers more than 30 countries and regions, including China, the Middle East, Africa, central Asia, southeast Asia and Latin America, forming a rapidly responsive global service support system.
Anton provides a full range of products and services in the oil and gas development process to meet the diverse needs of clients and help them maximize the value of oil and gas assets. Anton's comprehensive products and services include: products and services for the whole process of oil and gas development, a full range of professional resource services for oil and gas development, and integrated services in various forms.
Anton's corporate culture is rooted in the traditional oriental culture. Anton's core values are Client-centric, Hard working, Learning and innovation. Anton's mission is to help others succeed, share fruits with employees internally and achieve win-win development with partners externally.
Anton's vision is to be a model for the harmony and high-efficiency development between man and environment. In every construction work scene, Anton is committed to achieving the standard of "operation first" and creating value for clients to the maximum extent. In the process of development, Anton does the best to promote social progress and achieve harmonious development with the society.
descriptionHtml:
Job Responsibilities


1. Strategic Planning and Business Leadership: Based on global energy market trends and company strategy, formulate medium- and long-term development plans for exploration and development, lead the layout of emerging businesses such as unconventional oil and gas and offshore oilfields, and promote the implementation of the "Digital Oilfield" and "Green Oilfield" strategies. Lead oilfield development investment decisions and assess the feasibility of new projects (e.g., reservoir reserves, development costs, policy risks).


2. Full-Process Operation Management: Coordinate the entire oilfield exploration and development chain, optimize resource allocation, supervise project delivery on schedule, and ensure compliance with international standards such as ISO and API, as well as local environmental regulations. Establish a cost control system, focusing on cost reduction in equipment maintenance and technology outsourcing, and maximize customer asset value through full-chain service capabilities.


3. Risk and Compliance Management: Establish a project risk assessment mechanism to address external challenges such as oil price fluctuations, geopolitics, and resource depletion, ensuring the stable operation of overseas projects in Iraq, Chad, and other locations. Lead the construction of a QHSE (Quality, Health, Safety, and Environment) system, handle major safety incidents and customer complaints, and maintain the company's international reputation.


4. Client Collaboration and Team Building: Maintain strategic partnerships with international oil companies and local governments, promote the implementation of international cooperation projects, and regularly report business progress to the president of the oil and gas development company. Responsible for building a talent pool for oilfield evaluation, establishing a global technical training system, and cultivating cross-departmental, multi-skilled personnel.


Job Qualifications


1. Education and Professional Background: Master's degree or above in Geological Resources and Geological Engineering, Petroleum Engineering, Oil and Gas Field Development, or related fields.


2. Experience and Abilities: Over 20 years of experience across the entire oil and gas exploration and development chain, including at least 10 years of experience in core oilfield evaluation business (e.g., reserve assessment, development potential demonstration, block value evaluation); at least 10 years of mid-to-senior level management experience in oil and gas companies or internationally renowned oilfield service companies (e.g., director-level or above in charge of exploration and development); experience in managing large-scale global or cross-regional (e.g., Middle East, West Africa, Central Asia) oil and gas field projects (recoverable reserves ≥ 500 million barrels of oil equivalent); experience in building overseas oilfield evaluation teams and managing projects with international oil companies (IOCs) is a plus. Must possess the language skills required for global business, be proficient in reading and writing professional English reports (e.g., oil and gas development plan reports, oil and gas project feasibility analysis reports), and be able to conduct international negotiations and cross-cultural team communication in English as a working language. Arabic proficiency is preferred.



3. Other requirements: Priority will be given to candidates with a professor-level senior engineer title; priority will also be given to candidates holding relevant professional certifications such as Registered Petroleum Engineer (PE), Project Management Professional (PMP), or Specialized Petroleum Reserves Appraiser (SPE-PRB).

descriptionHtml:
About the Client

Our client is a leading global marketing and communications network with a strong footprint across the Middle East. Known for delivering integrated, data-driven, and creatively led solutions, the organization partners with some of the region’s most prominent brands and institutions.


The Role

Our client is seeking a commercially driven and strategically minded Managing Director to lead and expand operations in Saudi Arabia. This role requires a seasoned leader with deep market expertise, strong client relationships, and a proven track record of driving growth within the Kingdom.


The Managing Director will be responsible for overseeing business performance, leading multidisciplinary teams, strengthening client partnerships, and positioning the agency as a market leader in KSA.


Key Responsibilities

Strategic Leadership

  • Define and execute the agency’s vision and growth strategy in Saudi Arabia aligned with regional and global objectives
  • Identify new business opportunities, partnerships, and revenue streams within the KSA market
  • Act as a senior advisor to clients on brand, communications, and transformation strategies

Business Growth & Commercial Performance

  • Drive revenue growth, profitability, and overall financial performance
  • Lead new business development efforts, including pitching and client acquisition
  • Expand and diversify the client portfolio across key sectors in Saudi Arabia

Client Leadership

  • Build and maintain strong relationships with C-level stakeholders and key decision-makers
  • Ensure delivery of high-quality, integrated marketing and communications solutions
  • Champion client satisfaction, retention, and long-term partnerships

Operational Excellence

  • Oversee day-to-day operations, ensuring efficiency, scalability, and delivery excellence
  • Implement best practices across processes, governance, and performance management
  • Ensure alignment with regional and global standards

Team Leadership & Culture

  • Lead, inspire, and develop a high-performing team across strategy, creative, and client servicing
  • Foster a collaborative, innovative, and performance-driven culture
  • Attract, retain, and mentor top talent in the Saudi market

Market Expertise & Representation

  • Act as a key spokesperson and representative of the agency in Saudi Arabia
  • Leverage deep understanding of local culture, consumer behavior, and regulatory landscape
  • Stay ahead of industry trends, particularly in digital transformation, media, and communications


Key Requirements

  • 12–18+ years of experience in advertising, marketing, or communications, with significant leadership exposure
  • Proven experience leading business operations in Saudi Arabia or the GCC, with strong knowledge of the KSA market
  • Demonstrated success in driving revenue growth and managing P&L
  • Strong network of senior client relationships within Saudi Arabia
  • Experience leading integrated campaigns across creative, digital, media, and data
  • Exceptional leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills
  • Entrepreneurial mindset with a hands-on, results-driven approach
